name: Oven-Braised Pheasant with Sauerkraut
recipeIngredient: 3 lb Sauerkraut, thoroughly rinsed and drained
½ tsp Dried thyme or 1 sprig fresh thyme
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the pheasant by removing skin, fat, and cutting into serving pieces (including bones).
2. A favorite of Jean-Lours Gerin, this dish uses both legs and breasts for an elegant presentation.
3. Avoid canned sauerkraut with preservatives; opt for plain salt-cured varieties.
4. Preheat oven to 300°F. In a large Dutch oven, cook bacon until crisp (3-5 minutes).
5. Add pheasant legs, backs, and wings, browning in batches on all sides (3-4 minutes). Refrigerate breast pieces until needed.
6. Add sauerkraut, wine, juniper berries, thyme, cloves, and bay leaf to the Dutch oven. Cook over medium heat until about half the liquid has evaporated (10-15 minutes).
7. Place the Dutch oven in the oven and bake for about 2 hours, stirring occasionally and adding liquid as needed, until legs are tender and sauerkraut is slightly browned.
8. Remove from oven, discard backs, wings, and bay leaf, season to taste, cover, and keep warm.
9. Raise oven temperature to 425°F. Season pheasant breasts with salt and pepper, place on a rack in a roasting pan, and roast for 20-25 minutes until juices run clear when pierced with a fork.
10. Remove breasts from roasting pan and keep warm. Reheat legs briefly in the Dutch oven.
11. Slice meat from breasts and lay over legs and sauerkraut.
293 CALORIES PER SERVING: 40 G PROTEIN, 10 G FAT, 11 G CARBOHYDRATE; 840 MG SODIUM; 111 MG CHOLESTEROL.
